name: Turkestan Autonomous Soviet Socialist Republic (1918–1924)
description: A plain red field with the initials of the republic inscribed in gold in the upper hoist, rendered in both the reformed Arabic (Yaña imlâ) script and Russian Cyrillic ('ТССР').
history: The Turkestan ASSR, originally the Turkestan Soviet Federative Republic, was an autonomous republic of the Russian SFSR in Soviet Central Asia (1918–1924), spanning the territory of the later Uzbek, Turkmen, Tajik, Kyrgyz and Karakalpak republics. Its flag, dating from about 1919, was a red banner bearing the state initials in gold in the upper hoist in both Arabic and Cyrillic script; the republic was dissolved on 27 October 1924 in the national delimitation of Central Asia.

features.role: state initials in Arabic (Yaña imlâ) and Cyrillic ('ТССР')
features.symbolism: the name of the Turkestan Autonomous Soviet Socialist Republic
